metadata.purpose: Reusable indexed database of Joystick Jammers user journeys, contradictions, underspecification, win-condition rules, and acceptance tests.

topology.local: One host/big screen renders the world; phones/keyboards are controllers only.
topology.remote: Host remains authoritative; each participant renders a synced viewer on their own device.
topology.mixed: A Local couch room also has remote viewers/drivers or paired screen/controller seats.

role.host: Authoritative simulator and canonical room owner.
role.driver: Owns a car/seat and sends input.
role.viewer: Renders synced world state but does not mutate seat state.

status.current_with_gap: Implemented enough to exist, but has known user-flow or reliability gaps.
status.planned: Designed in plans but not implemented.
status.recommended: Recommended by this database to resolve an underspecified journey.
status.missing: Expected by the target experience, but no meaningful implementation was found.
status.contradiction: Current code/docs conflict with desired behavior or with each other.
status.underspecified: A decision is needed before implementation.

flows.title: Host creates a Local TV room
preconditions: Host browser can run WebGL and Socket.IO
happy_path: Host opens the host screen.
Room is created and a 4-character room code plus QR/join URL are displayed.
Host remains in lobby until players join and settings are chosen.
branches: Room creation times out.
Explicit Local/Remote choice is planned but not current.
Host network blip tries reclaim_room, but current server host-loss semantics conflict.

flows.title: Host creates Remote room as driver-viewer
happy_path: Host chooses Remote before room creation.
Server creates a remote topology room.
Host gets a driver seat and viewer camera.
Invite panel exposes copy link, share, and QR.
branches: Fallback to pure host screen if host-as-player is too heavy.
Public Remote requires security hardening before internet use.

flows.title: Same-room phone joins as controller via QR
happy_path: Player scans QR or opens prefilled player page.
Room code is normalized and shown.
Player confirms or edits name.
Server joins player to waiting room.
Phone shows waiting screen and controller later.
branches: Current auto-join can skip the intended name confirmation.
Invalid/expired room returns friendly retry state.
QR for join must be visually and semantically distinct from pair QR.

flows.title: Manual room-code join
preconditions: Player has code from host screen
happy_path: Player types or pastes room code.
Code is normalized uppercase.
Player enters or accepts name.
Join succeeds and waiting screen appears.
branches: Lowercase and pasted punctuation are normalized.
Unknown room shows actionable error.
Path-style /join/CODE is planned but not implemented.

flows.title: Invite link chooses controller, remote screen, or spectator
happy_path: Invitee opens shared link.
Chooser asks whether they can see the host screen.
Yes routes to controller-only.
No routes to viewer/driver or paired-phone flow.
Optional watch-only routes to spectator if enabled.
branches: QR scan with via=qr skips chooser to controller-only.
Desktop defaults to screen-here.
Phone defaults to controller-only.
Invalid/expired/finished rooms show useful CTAs.

flows.title: Laptop viewer pairs with phone controller for one seat
happy_path: Viewer device joins or creates one seat.
Server mints short-lived pair code.
Phone scans pair QR or enters pair code.
Phone becomes sole controller binding for the same seat.
Viewer renders seat camera and all world state.
branches: Phone-first pairing is symmetric.
Pair code expires or is already used.
Second controller attempts takeover.
Viewer disconnects while controller remains active.

flows.title: Pure viewer or spectator joins
happy_path: Viewer joins without a car.
Server binds viewer role only.
Viewer receives seeded arena and transforms.
Camera defaults to auto/free/spectator mode.
branches: Upgrade to driver if room allows.
Spectate when room is finished or full.
Eliminated derby players become spectators.

flows.title: Lobby readiness, settings, and min-player gates
happy_path: Players join and see identity.
Host selects ruleset, track/arena, laps/round settings.
Settings lock when countdown begins.
Start is allowed only when ruleset-specific minimums are met.
branches: Race can support one player for practice.
Derby should require at least two active drivers or a practice label.
Late joiners in lobby inherit current settings.

flows.title: Name, color, number, and car identity mirrored everywhere
happy_path: Seat receives distinct number/color/name.
Phone shows the same identity as TV/viewer.
Host render shows readable label/number/own-car cue.
Results use identity instead of raw player ID.
branches: Beyond color-count, number/pattern becomes primary.
Disconnected seat uses away/ghost visual.
Spectators see identities without needing a controller.

flows.title: First-run tutorial and control scheme choice
happy_path: Player sees scheme-appropriate ghost gestures.
Tutorial teaches by doing, then fades.
Completion persists but can be replayed.
branches: Classic touch controls.
Keyboard desktop controls.
Twin-stick or alternate scheme later.
Reduced-motion users get non-animated affordance.

flows.title: Controller input confidence and reconnect feel
happy_path: Controls send at fixed cadence.
Connection indicator reflects connected/reconnecting.
Controls zero when tab hides.
Reconnect attempts to restore seat.
branches: Input ack/latency not currently visible.
Wheelie/handling states need clearer reduced-authority cues.
Keyboard handling should route through canonical mapper.

flows.title: Countdown visible to host, controllers, and viewers
happy_path: Host sees countdown.
Controllers switch to game controls.
Remote viewers show synchronized countdown.
Input is ignored or queued until Go.
branches: Test mode skips countdown.
Derby has per-round countdown.
Reconnect during countdown lands in correct screen.

flows.title: Late race join before first finisher
preconditions: Race has not entered finish grace
happy_path: Late player joins.
Controller skips waiting room.
Host spawns car near last place or last checkpoint.
Player is marked late/new.
branches: Spawn must be safe and on ground.
Late player may need catch-up or ineligible podium flag.
Remote late join needs seed and current snapshot.

flows.title: Late race join during finish grace or results
preconditions: First finisher exists or host is on results
happy_path: Joiner is not eligible to affect winner/podium.
Joiner may spectate results or queue for next race.
Server and host agree on room state.
branches: If all players returned to lobby, join as normal lobby player.
If host has closed, show ended-room CTA.

flows.title: First finisher starts visible grace timer; DNF resolves results
happy_path: First vehicle finish immediately establishes current winner.
Visible finish-grace timer starts on host/controllers/viewers.
Other racers can finish during grace.
When grace expires, unfinished racers become DNF ranked by progress.
Results are shown without waiting for last place.
branches: All vehicles finish before grace expires.
Host loss during grace pauses/resumes timer.
Disconnected players are DNF or away per reconnect policy.
Late join during grace queues/spectates.

flows.title: Race finish-time ties and dead heats
preconditions: Two or more vehicles finish within tie epsilon
happy_path: Finish times within epsilon share placement or use a declared deterministic tie-break.
Results explain the tie clearly.
Progress/DNF ranking cannot overtake a finisher.
branches: Network/remote viewer interpolation does not decide authority.
Photo-finish visual can be added later.

flows.title: Derby elimination round loop
preconditions: At least two active drivers recommended
happy_path: Countdown starts round.
Combat runs until one survivor remains.
Round scores are assigned.
Next round starts after inter-round delay.
Match ends at rounds-to-win or max rounds.
branches: One-player derby should be blocked or labeled practice.
All eliminated simultaneously needs tie policy.
Disconnects need explicit round policy.

flows.title: Late derby join waits or spectates until next round
happy_path: Late joiner enters as spectator or queued driver.
They see they will join the next round.
At next round reset, they spawn with everyone else.
branches: Host may allow immediate join only in explicit chaos/practice setting.
If match is near final result, queue for next match.

flows.title: Derby passive/stuck standoff reaches sudden death
preconditions: Two or more survivors remain too long or no damage occurs
happy_path: Round has max duration and/or no-damage timer.
Warning escalates before timeout.
Arena shrink, hazard, or damage-over-time forces resolution.
If still unresolved, score by health/damage/position with declared tie rules.
branches: Accessibility/reduced chaos setting can use score timeout instead of harsh hazard.
Disconnected cars follow derby disconnect policy.

flows.title: Simultaneous derby elimination and tie policy
preconditions: All remaining cars are destroyed in same resolution window
happy_path: Game uses a defined simultaneity window.
Round is draw or winner is chosen by health-before-hit/damage-dealt/tiebreak rule.
Results explain why.
branches: Two-player mutual destruction.
Multi-car explosion chain.

flows.title: Podium, social stats, rematch, and back-to-lobby
happy_path: Host and viewers show podium/results.
Controllers show result state and rematch/lobby status.
Results include identity, DNF status, and useful stats.
Host chooses Play Again or Back to Lobby.
branches: Remote rematch may use vote/ready-up later.
Disconnected players should be shown as away/DNF, not invisible.
Back to lobby resets server state and controller screens.

flows.title: Spectating after elimination, finish, or waiting for next round
preconditions: Player has no active driving agency in current phase
happy_path: Finished/eliminated player sees clear status.
They can watch without controlling their car.
They know when they will drive again.
branches: Race finisher during grace watches placements resolve.
Derby eliminated player spectates until next round.
Pure spectators never get a car.

flows.title: Host drops, room pauses, host reclaims or room ends cleanly
happy_path: Server marks host lost instead of deleting room.
Players/viewers see paused host-lost message.
Host reclaims within grace and room resumes.
If timeout expires, room ends with useful CTAs.
branches: Local couch transient Wi-Fi loss.
Remote host loss during race finish grace.
Future host migration can build on snapshot.

flows.title: Intentional leave is distinct from transient disconnect
happy_path: Intentional leave confirms removal or DNF.
Transient drop creates away/ghost seat for timeout window.
Reconnect restores same seat if within policy.
branches: Lobby leave removes immediately.
Race leave during grace becomes DNF.
Derby leave may eliminate or queue next round.
Viewer disconnect does not affect controller seat.

flows.title: Same seat opened in two tabs or devices
preconditions: Existing seat token or reconnect id is reused
happy_path: Server allows exactly one active controller binding per seat.
Second controller is rejected or explicitly takes over.
Old controller receives clear message if replaced.
branches: Viewer duplicates are allowed if read-only.
Controller takeover may require confirmation.
Expired token cannot claim seat.

flows.title: Invalid, expired, ended, full, or unavailable room handling
happy_path: Error copy names the state accurately.
Screen offers retry, create room, or spectate when applicable.
Join button re-enables and focus returns to useful input.
branches: Code malformed.
Room not found.
Host left.
Room ended.
Public Remote throttled.

flows.title: Moderation, grief, and public Remote abuse flows
happy_path: Host can kick or mute a disruptive seat.
Names are sanitized and length-limited server-side.
Reset spam and input spoofing are rejected/rate-limited.
Blocked player sees clear terminal state.
branches: Local trusted couch can keep shorter code.
Public Remote needs longer codes and rate limits.
Spectators should not send driver actions.

flows.title: Host closes, room expires, and abandoned rooms are reaped
happy_path: Server tracks created_at and last_activity.
Empty waiting rooms expire.
Finished abandoned rooms expire after results grace.
Later join attempts get expired-room copy.
branches: Server restart kills in-memory rooms in v1.
Public Remote may need persistence later.

flows.title: Host restart, back-to-lobby, and reset-all are confirmed and announced
happy_path: Host opens menu.
Destructive actions require confirm or hold.
Players/viewers see announcement before state changes.
Server state transitions match host UI.
branches: Reset all cars during race.
Restart current ruleset.
Back to lobby from results or mid-match.
Report bug is non-destructive.

issues.summary: The term mode is overloaded between Local/Remote topology and Race/Derby ruleset.
issues.recommended_resolution: Use topology_kind for local/remote/mixed and ruleset for race/derby in new docs, events, and tests.

issues.summary: Race results wait for every registered vehicle to finish, so last place/AFK can block the winner.
issues.recommended_resolution: First finisher starts a visible grace timer; unfinished players become DNF ranked by progress when it expires.

issues.summary: Host emits end_game but no server handler was found, so server finished-room state is likely not authoritative.
issues.recommended_resolution: Add host-authorized end_game handling and define join behavior for finished/finish-grace/results states.

issues.summary: Planned host-loss grace conflicts with current immediate room deletion.
issues.recommended_resolution: Mark host lost, pause room, preserve snapshot and seats, allow timed reclaim, then expire cleanly.

issues.summary: Reconnect claims are stronger than implementation: current flow stores session ID but host removes vehicles on disconnect.
issues.recommended_resolution: Move to durable seat tokens; show disconnected cars as away/transparent; reconnect restores same seat and live state.

issues.summary: First-run QR/code path can auto-join before the player confirms name, conflicting with the README journey.
issues.recommended_resolution: Prefill room and random name, but require a clear Join confirmation on first run.

issues.summary: Remote viewer path is planned but lacks ViewerGameHost, full transforms, and required seed.
issues.recommended_resolution: Implement shared seed, transform payload, separate viewer renderer, and interpolation before Remote play.

issues.summary: Mixed Local plus Remote friend conflicts with docs that make Local/Remote a fixed room-wide mode.
issues.recommended_resolution: Make viewer fanout a seat/room capability decision or explicitly disallow mixed topology in v1.

issues.summary: Two-device one-seat requires seat registry; current server model is socket keyed player storage.
issues.recommended_resolution: Introduce seat registry keyed by durable player/seat id with controller_sid and viewer_sids.

issues.summary: Control updates trust client-supplied player_id; public Remote and paired seats are unsafe.
issues.recommended_resolution: Authorize input by request.sid owning the controller binding for that seat; validate host-only state broadcasts.

issues.summary: Late derby joiners currently enter active combat rounds, which is unfair and hard to explain.
issues.recommended_resolution: Queue late derby joiners as spectators until next round unless host explicitly enables chaos join.

issues.summary: Derby disconnect policy is inconsistent and may leave stale survivor registration.
issues.recommended_resolution: Define drop policy: ghost timeout, auto-eliminate, or queue next round; ensure derby unregister/eliminate is coherent.

issues.summary: Derby has no stalemate/no-damage timer; passive survivors can run indefinitely.
issues.recommended_resolution: Add max round/no-damage timer with sudden death, shrink escalation, or score timeout.

issues.summary: Derby tie behavior is not fully specified and may pick first iterated player before points tiebreak.
issues.recommended_resolution: Define deterministic tie order and simultaneous elimination policy.

issues.summary: Phone/controller clients lack clear game-end and returned-to-lobby transitions.
issues.recommended_resolution: Controllers should show results/status/lobby states and reset controls when host transitions.

issues.summary: Results UI renders player IDs rather than names/colors/numbers, weakening social payoff.
issues.recommended_resolution: Results payload/UI should include display name, number, color, DNF and relevant stats.

issues.summary: Host restart, back-to-lobby, and reset-all are immediate actions without confirmation or player announcement.
issues.recommended_resolution: Require confirm/hold for destructive actions and broadcast pending/complete state to controllers/viewers.

issues.summary: Spectator role is implied but not a defined server/client role.
issues.recommended_resolution: Decide whether spectators ship in v1, then define join, camera, upgrade, and result behavior.

issues.summary: Room-code copy alternates between letters-only and alphanumeric.
issues.recommended_resolution: Choose copy and validation language that match generated codes.

issues.summary: Finished-late-join behavior is important and explicitly skipped in current E2E.
issues.recommended_resolution: Implement and test join during finish grace/results.

issues.summary: Keyboard/controller handling is duplicated and not a first-class scheme.
issues.recommended_resolution: Route touch and keyboard through ControlMapper/ControlScheme and define focus/legend behavior.

issues.summary: Public Remote lacks rate limit, name sanitation, and moderation flow.
issues.recommended_resolution: Add join rate limits, longer public codes, server-side name sanitation, and host moderation actions.

issues.summary: One-player Derby is not gated or explained.
issues.recommended_resolution: Require two active drivers for normal Derby, or label and handle solo practice explicitly.

issues.summary: Late join after first finisher must not affect winner/podium.
issues.recommended_resolution: Once finish grace starts, late joiners are spectator/queued and ineligible for current results.

assertions: Host receives valid room code
Join QR/link targets controller-only Local route
Room code is prefilled
Player can confirm/edit name before first join
No accidental auto-join on first run
Invalid/not-found/expired/ended states use distinct copy
Retry/create/spectate actions are available when appropriate
QR path skips chooser to controller-only
Plain invite link shows can-you-see-host-screen chooser
Viewer and phone share one player_id
Only phone/controller binding can send driving input
Pair code is single-use and expires
Host and viewer use same seed/arena
Viewer receives all car transforms
Own car is identifiable
Remote host has a driver seat
Host keyboard/touch input controls only host seat
Other viewers see host car move
Normal Derby cannot start with fewer than two active drivers or is explicitly marked practice
Host, controllers, and remote viewers see synchronized countdown
Reconnect during countdown lands in correct state
Late joiner spawns on drivable ground
Late joiner is marked late/new
Existing racers are not disrupted
Late join during finish grace cannot affect winner/podium
Joiner becomes spectator or queued for next race
First finisher starts visible grace
AFK/stuck last place becomes DNF by progress
Results do not wait forever
Finish-time epsilon produces declared tie or deterministic tiebreak
DNF cannot outrank finisher
Host-authorized end_game sets server room finished
Join after results is rejected, queued, or spectator-only by spec
Mid-round derby joiner spectates or waits
They spawn only at next round reset
Two passive survivors cannot remain in combat forever
Sudden-death/score-timeout is visible and deterministic
Simultaneous elimination window is deterministic
Round draw or winner by tiebreak is explicit
Disconnected derby player does not remain an unkillable survivor
Reconnect/eliminate/queue policy is applied
Controller shows match ended/results status
Back to lobby returns phone to waiting state
Play again returns phone to countdown/game state
Results show names, colors, numbers, and DNF status
Raw playerId is not the primary public label
Host disconnect pauses room without deleting it
Host reclaim within grace restores players
Timeout ends room cleanly
Mid-race drop shows away/transparent car
Reconnect restores same seat
Timeout applies DNF/eliminate/remove policy
Two tabs cannot both control the same seat
Takeover or rejection is explicit
Socket cannot drive another player_id
Viewer cannot send driver input
Fake host vehicle_states are dropped
Server normalizes name length/content
Lobby/results render names safely
Rejected names produce friendly copy
Restart/back-to-lobby/reset-all require confirm/hold
Controllers/viewers receive announcement
Idle/finished/host-lost rooms expire by policy
Late join after reaping gets expired/not-found CTA

decisions.summary: Should mixed Local plus remote viewer/driver be supported as a seat capability, or should Local/Remote remain mutually exclusive room topologies in v1?
decisions.recommended: Support it as a seat/viewer capability if Remote is being built anyway; otherwise explicitly defer mixed topology and keep invite copy honest.

decisions.summary: What is the Race finish-grace duration and DNF ranking formula?
decisions.recommended: Use a configurable visible grace window, initially 30-45 seconds or a percentage of leader time; rank unfinished by lap, checkpoint, then distance to next checkpoint.

decisions.summary: Do spectators ship in Remote v1?
decisions.recommended: Define spectator role now even if UI is hidden; it is needed for late joins, eliminated derby players, and finish-grace viewers.

decisions.summary: What should happen to a derby player who disconnects mid-round?
decisions.recommended: Mark away briefly, then auto-eliminate or queue for next round; never leave the seat as a counted immortal survivor.

decisions.summary: Should QR joins auto-submit or require first-run confirmation?
decisions.recommended: QR should prefill the room and name, but first run should require an obvious Join confirmation; repeat reconnect can remain one-tap.
